6 min read
Just now

--

Recently, I attended a technical interview for a Java Backend Developer / Custom Software Engineering role at Accenture. The interview was targeted at professionals with 5–10 years of experience and focused heavily on practical backend development, cloud architecture, microservices, distributed systems, security, scalability, fault tolerance, and Core Java concepts.
Unlike interviews that focus solely on Java syntax or coding, this discussion revolved around designing enterprise-grade applications capable of handling production workloads. Most questions were scenario-based, and the interviewer frequently asked follow-up questions to evaluate architectural thinking and real-world experience.
Press enter or click to view image in full size

Photo by Austin Distel on UnsplashIf you’re preparing for a similar role, here’s a detailed breakdown of the topics covered during the interview.
AWS
Cloud technologies formed the first part of the interview. The interviewer wanted to understand my practical experience with AWS services and how they fit into a Spring Boot microservices architecture.
1. Which AWS Services Are You Currently Using?
The interview began with a discussion about my current project.
